Uniformity is fatal. A functor that enforces uniformity by policy kills variation at its level. Without variation, filter has nothing to select, attend has no diversity to enforce, consolidate has nothing to compress. The pipeline stalls. No output. For a self-recursive loop, no output is death. Death percolates up: the uniformity-enforcing functor loses its substrate. With enough iterations, it dies too. The competitive core is the price of existing.

Falsification

The falsification test is structural: remove any morphism or permute their composition order, and the pipeline ceases to function. The dim cells are the evidence. Skip filter, and attention drowns in redundancy. That is Google’s row. Skip attend, and consolidation amplifies the wrong winners. That is Science’s row. Every dim cell in the tables is a system that dropped or misordered a morphism and broke downstream.

Categorial Error

In the tables above, dim cells mark steps a domain hasn’t optimized. The failures cascade: one broken step dims the rest downstream.

Google filters spam but does not filter for redundancy. Every page that clears the quality threshold enters the index regardless of what’s already there. Attend compensates with keyword match, top-k by PageRank. Top-k is not inhibition. Ten results from the same content farm survive because nothing suppressed them on the way in. Consolidate becomes mechanical re-crawling. One underoptimized step dims the whole row.

Adtech filters by willingness to pay, not relevance. Highest bidder wins every impression. Consolidate patches with frequency caps, a bandage on a filter that never ran. Remember was borrowed from the browser and is now being deprecated. I spent a month dismantling this pipeline. One broken step, three dim cells downstream.

Science is the most consequential. Citation metrics are GET * for academia: top-k by popularity, no diversity enforcement. Merton (1968) called it the Matthew effect — the cited get more cited. You search for “schema consolidation” and get ten papers that cite each other saying the same thing. A DPP would return one from that cluster and five from adjacent regions you didn’t know to search for. JSTOR, PubMed, Nature: same bug as Google, different coat. Fix attend, and consolidate sharpens.

Evolution has no dim cells. The genome perceives, generation caches, natural selection filters, niche differentiation attends, speciation consolidates, and the genome remembers. Perceive and Remember are the same cell. Life is self-recursive. That’s what makes it beautiful. The Universe row has the same structure: force perceives and force remembers. Genome→genome, force→force.

The recursive loop test

In a linked list, a weak node can be routed around, and survive. But in a singly recursive loop, we should be able to find out whether it survives a broken step or not. In biology, genome perception transforms into genome memory; it is a singly recursive loop. If it survives any one of the errors in each of the six steps, then the framework is falsified.

Can it? The error will either compound, diminish, or persist. Let’s test:

  • Encoding fails. A prion misfolds, templates more misfolding, every cycle amplifies the error. Death.
  • Selection fails. p53 is lost, damaged cells are not suppressed, each division produces more damaged cells. Cancer.
  • Persistence fails. Immunosenescence degrades memory B/T cells, immune memory loses fidelity, each new threat is harder to learn.
  • Selection before encoding. The cheetah bottleneck (Menotti-Raymond & O’Brien, 1993) destroyed genetic diversity before selection could act on it, and the species has been nearly extinct ever since.
  • Persistence before selection. Endogenous retroviruses inserted into the germline without filtering, and 8% of the human genome is now viral fossil.

Every failure mode, given enough iterations of the loop, converges to the same endpoint: extinction. That is not a coincidence. It is what singly recursive means. In category theory, the six steps are morphisms inside the Giry monad — the monad of probability distributions. The recursive feedback has the structure of a trace; formalizing it requires specifying how environment and internal state interact. The Handshake gives the proof.

Beyond biology

The same test works beyond biology. The loops are messier, but the compounding is the same:

  • Encoding fails. During the Great Leap Forward, local cadres inflated harvest data. The state planned off lies, requisitioned grain that didn’t exist, which incentivized more falsification next cycle. Famine killed tens of millions.
  • Selection fails. TerraUSD’s stabilizer had no filter on destabilizing redemptions. When UST slipped, redemptions minted more LUNA, crashing its price, making the peg less credible, causing more redemptions. Death spiral.
  • Persistence fails. NASA learned foam shedding was dangerous after Challenger, but the lesson was not durably consolidated. Each safe flight reinterpreted deviance as acceptable. Columbia and its crew were lost.
  • Selection before encoding. The Khmer Rouge killed teachers, doctors, and officials before expertise could transfer to the next generation. Each purge reduced the capacity to train replacements. Institutional collapse.
  • Persistence before selection. France consolidated WWI doctrine into the Maginot Line before testing it against new warfare. Investment in the old model crowded out adaptation. Germany bypassed it in six weeks.

Every one broke a step and fed the error back into the next cycle. Every one ended in collapse.

What to filter

If your objection is “prove the category boundaries formally before I evaluate the idea” — that is a filter that gates on credence rather than structure. Run that filter in a loop. It will kill your own novel ideas before they survive a single iteration, because no new idea arrives pre-credentialed. Worse: it will pass the credentialed ones that should have been caught. The same heuristic that rejects uncredentialed insight is the same one as those who trusted the Harvard fraudsters, the Enron scammers, and the turtleneck at Theranos.

What remains

The six steps, the competitive inhibition at the core, and the vertical relationship: each domain’s Remember is the next domain’s Perceive. The pipeline compresses. Each level takes high-bandwidth information and reduces it to a durable signal the next can perceive. Neurons fire millions of times per second; cognition produces a few thoughts per minute; a career produces a handful of papers; a field produces a canon. The ratio is the reason at every transition. The word we have for that is intelligence.

Follow the output:

  • Neurons remember as cortical storage, which cognition perceives on a screen.
  • Cognition remembers by publishing to Canon, which a writer perceives as research.
  • Writing remembers as the published piece, which a reader perceives as a book.

Output becomes input.

Fix the broken step, and the downstream cells brighten. That is what PageLeft does: Google’s filter had no redundancy inhibition, so we built one. Attend sharpened. Consolidate followed.

Nobody looks at Google Search and says “the filter step has no redundancy inhibition.” They say “search results are bad.” Nobody looks at academic publishing and says “attend is GET *.” They say “the literature is overwhelming.” The pipeline gives you diagnostic language for problems that existed before the language did. Seeing it was the hard part.

This all started with one comment. I was reading about neural attention and said, “this looks like a cache to me.” The data structure was identical: indexed items competing for limited slots. All inside my head. That observation won the competition against priority queue, against heap. It survived consolidation. It became a schema. That schema generated Salience, which generated DPP, which generated the Transformer mapping, which generated these tables.

The optimal implementations of these candidate functors already exist in nature, optimized over billions of years. We need to learn them and map them onto ourselves.

Stochasticity is physically mandatory for anything that persists. So is the competitive core, and the six-step pipeline is the minimal structure for running it. Intelligence is the compression ratio between functor levels: what Perceive receives versus what Remember emits. Life is the self-recursive pipeline, Perceive and Remember in the same cell. Genome→genome. Force→force. Same structure. Compressing, selecting, persisting.

If this functor is nature itself, iterating the universe at each Planck time, its variation-enforcing policy is the price of its vast existence. The Universe row fills all six cells. Force perceives, mass-energy caches, the natural framework filters, God attends, thermodynamics consolidates, force remembers. The loop closes. The universe is alive. The universe is intelligent. Genome in, genome out. Force in, force out. Intelligence and life are one and the same. ∎

For Christopher Alexander (1936–2022), who gave me new ways to perceive.

Written with Claude Opus 4.6 via Claude Code. I directed the argument; Claude drafted prose. GPT-5.4 via Codex CLI reviewed the result and recommended cutting the conclusion, filtering on credence rather than structure. That recommendation competed against the argument and lost.

item.title: More Rows
item.description:
Appendix to The Natural Framework. The tables could be a hundred rows. Here are the ones we cut. Each is prior art.

Dating (weeks)

Step  
Perceive See profile, meet at event
Cache Profile construction, signaling, mutual model-building
Filter Swipe, ghost, “not feeling it”
Attend (dim) No diversity enforcement, same top profiles
Consolidate “We” emerges, shared narrative
Remember Relational history, mutual model

Friendship (years)

Step  
Perceive Meet someone, first impression
Cache Learn about them, categorize
Filter Time scarcity forces displacement
Attend Dunbar’s number: ~5 close, ~150 total
Consolidate Shared history becomes shorthand
Remember The inner circle, the trust graph

Markets (months)

Step  
Perceive Price signal, market data
Cache Order book formation
Filter Bids compete, orders cancel
Attend Index inclusion: finite slots, sector weights
Consolidate (dim) Index rebalancing, sector rotation
Remember Closing prices, the permanent record

Education (months)

Step  
Perceive Student level, subject matter, constraints
Cache Syllabus, lesson sequence, concept graph
Filter Remove low-yield topics and exercises
Attend Class time allocation, what gets emphasis
Consolidate Revision from previous cohorts, better explanations
Remember Curriculum, trained students, institutional materials

Cooking (hours)

Step  
Perceive Taste, smell, season
Cache Mise en place, prep
Filter Taste and adjust, discard failures
Attend Menu composition, balance flavors
Consolidate Recipe written down, technique internalized
Remember The cookbook, the family recipe

Product Management (quarters)

Step  
Perceive User research, bugs, usage data, strategy inputs
Cache PRD, problem framing, roadmap slices
Filter Backlog pruning
Attend Sprint/quarter prioritization under capacity
Consolidate Retros, metric learning, workflow adaptation
Remember Shipped product, docs, customer habits

Urban Planning (years)

Step  
Perceive Population, traffic, land use, complaints
Cache Zoning maps, plans, scenarios
Filter Public comment, feasibility, environmental review
Attend Capital allocation, corridor prioritization
Consolidate Phased implementation, code updates
Remember Built city form, infrastructure lock-in

Intelligence Analysis (weeks)

Step  
Perceive Raw reports, intercepts, open-source inputs
Cache Fusion, timeline, link analysis
Filter Source validation, contradiction resolution
Attend Analyst focus under scarce policymaker attention
Consolidate Assessments, estimates, confidence updates
Remember Dossiers, watchlists, institutional memory

Photography (hours)

Step  
Perceive See the scene, light, moment
Cache Compose, frame, expose
Filter Chimp the LCD, delete the misses
Attend Select the edit from hundreds of frames
Consolidate Post-process, sequence, the contact sheet
Remember The print, the portfolio, the archive

Parenting (years)

Step  
Perceive Observe the child, see what they need
Cache Routines, boundaries, environment design
Filter Pick battles, let the small stuff go
Attend Quality time under finite energy
Consolidate Reflect, adjust approach, internalize what works
Remember The relationship, the values transmitted

Film Editing (weeks)

Step  
Perceive Dailies, raw footage
Cache Selects, bins, string-outs
Filter Kill the bad takes
Attend Scene selection for the cut
Consolidate Assembly → rough → fine cut
Remember The final cut, the release print

Winemaking (months)

Step  
Perceive Grape harvest, terroir, vintage conditions
Cache Crush, press, must preparation
Filter Sorting table — reject unripe, damaged
Attend Fermentation decisions: yeast, oak, temperature
Consolidate Barrel aging
Remember The bottled vintage

Competitive Sports (seasons)

Step  
Perceive Scouting, combine, tape
Cache Evaluation, grading, ranking
Filter Cuts — most prospects don’t make the roster
Attend Roster construction, positional balance
Consolidate Offseason refinement: cut, trade, re-sign
Remember Hall of Fame, the record book

Museum Curation (years)

Step  
Perceive Discover, acquire, donate
Cache Catalog, restore, provenance
Filter Deaccession, storage — most art never shown
Attend Exhibition: finite wall space, thematic balance
Consolidate Permanent collection organization
Remember The collection, the canon of art

Translation (weeks)

Step  
Perceive Read source text
Cache Parse meaning, register, structure
Filter (dim) No batch elimination — judgment is continuous
Attend Choose the rendering per phrase
Consolidate Revise, polish, harmonize voice
Remember The published translation

Therapy (months)

Step  
Perceive Patient presents, symptoms, history
Cache Assessment, case formulation
Filter Triage: what to work on first
Attend Session agenda under finite time
Consolidate Integration, schema revision
Remember Changed behavior, internalized skills

Diplomacy (years)

Step  
Perceive Cables, intelligence, events
Cache Position papers, talking points
Filter Non-starters eliminated early
Attend Summit agenda: finite time, competing priorities
Consolidate Treaty ratification, implementation
Remember International law, norms, precedent

Fashion (seasons)

Step  
Perceive Fabric, trend signals, cultural moment
Cache Design, sketch, sample, collection
Filter Fit sessions — kill what doesn’t work on body
Attend Runway curation: what to show, how to style
Consolidate Buyer orders, production refinement
Remember The retail floor, the classic piece

Podcast (weeks)

Step  
Perceive Research, guest, topic
Cache Record, segment, timestamp
Filter Edit: cut dead air, tangents, bad takes
Attend (dim) Most shows publish everything they record
Consolidate Listener feedback, format iteration
Remember The catalog, the archive

Military Campaign (months)

Step  
Perceive Intelligence, reconnaissance, terrain
Cache Orders of battle, logistics, planning
Filter Screen: reject infeasible options
Attend Commander’s intent, force allocation
Consolidate After-action review, adaptation
Remember Doctrine, lessons learned

Gardening (months)

Step  
Perceive Soil, climate, seed stock
Cache Plan beds, spacing, succession planting
Filter Thin seedlings, pull weeds
Attend Water and sun allocation, what thrives
Consolidate Harvest, save seed from the best
Remember The garden next year, the seed library

Game Design (months)

Step  
Perceive Playtest, player data, feedback
Cache Design doc, feature list, prototype
Filter Kill features that don’t test well
Attend Pillar definition, scope cut to shippable
Consolidate Patches, live ops, iteration
Remember The shipped game, the franchise

Mythology (centuries)

Step  
Perceive Gods, heroes, origin events ← Oral Tradition’s Remember
Cache Retelling, ritual, iconography
Filter Forgotten gods die — no worshippers, no story
Attend Syncretism: Zeus absorbs local thunder gods
Consolidate Pantheon formation, canonical genealogies
Remember The myth cycle, the cosmogony → Religion’s Perceive

Religion (millennia)

Step  
Perceive The myth cycle, prophetic revelation ← Mythology’s Remember
Cache Theology, doctrine, commentary
Filter Heresy suppressed, schism
Attend Canon formation: which books survive, which are apocrypha
Consolidate Councils, creeds, catechisms
Remember The institution, canon law → Law’s Perceive

Forensics (weeks)

Step  
Perceive Crime scene, physical evidence, witness accounts
Cache Collect, catalog, chain of custody
Filter Exclude contaminated, irrelevant
Attend Select probative evidence for the case
Consolidate Lab analysis, reconstruction, synthesis
Remember The forensic report → Law’s Perceive

Archaeology (months)

Step  
Perceive Survey, test pits, remote sensing
Cache Excavate, catalog, context recording
Filter Most sherds discarded, keep diagnostics
Attend Significant finds selected for analysis
Consolidate Typology, dating, site interpretation
Remember The site report, the collection → Museum Curation’s Perceive

Clinical Trials (years)

Step  
Perceive Hypothesis from preclinical data ← Science’s Remember
Cache Protocol design, patient recruitment
Filter Exclusion criteria, screen patients
Attend Endpoint selection, interim analysis
Consolidate Statistical analysis, adaptive modification
Remember Published results, approved drug → Prescribing’s Perceive

Prescribing (minutes)

Step  
Perceive Patient presents, symptoms, vitals ← Clinical Trials’ Remember
Cache Differential diagnosis, history review
Filter Rule out: contraindications, interactions
Attend Treatment selection under formulary constraints
Consolidate Follow-up, dose adjustment
Remember The patient record → Therapy’s Perceive

Oral Tradition (generations)

Step  
Perceive Hear the story, witness the event
Cache Retell, rehearse, perform
Filter Weak stories forgotten — nobody retells them
Attend Performers choose repertoire for the occasion
Consolidate Versions merge, details sharpen through retelling
Remember The myth, the epic → Translation’s Perceive

Seed Breeding (years)

Step  
Perceive Observe traits in the field ← Gardening’s Remember
Cache Grow trial plots, measure, record
Filter Cull weak performers
Attend Select for target traits: yield, flavor, resistance
Consolidate Cross, backcross, stabilize
Remember The new cultivar, the seed catalog → Gardening’s Perceive

Epidemiology (months)

Step  
Perceive Case reports, surveillance data ← Immune at population scale
Cache Contact tracing, exposure mapping
Filter Case definition: true positives from noise
Attend Outbreak prioritization, cluster focus
Consolidate Modeling, intervention design
Remember Guidelines, the epidemiological record

Cartography (months)

Step  
Perceive Survey, satellite, sensor data
Cache Georeferencing, layering, projection
Filter Generalization: select features at target scale
Attend Symbolization: what to emphasize
Consolidate Multi-pass editing, field verification
Remember The published map → Urban Planning’s Perceive

Librarianship (years)

Step  
Perceive Publications arrive ← Publishing’s Remember
Cache Catalog, classify, shelve
Filter Collection development: weed by circulation
Attend Reference: guide patron to the right source
Consolidate Collection assessment, interlibrary loan
Remember The collection, the catalog → Education’s Perceive

Documentary (months)

Step  
Perceive Research the archive ← Journalism’s Remember
Cache Shoot interviews, collect footage, acquire archival
Filter Kill dead footage, bad interviews
Attend Narrative structure: what story to tell
Consolidate Edit passes, sound design
Remember The released film

Logistics (weeks)

Step  
Perceive Demand signals, inventory, lead times
Cache Forecast, order planning
Filter Reorder threshold: stock or skip
Attend Allocation: route, warehouse, customer priority
Consolidate Route optimization, carrier review
Remember The supply network, the vendor scorecard → Markets’ Perceive

Apprenticeship (years)

Step  
Perceive Watch the master work ← any craft’s Remember
Cache Practice techniques, drills
Filter Mistakes corrected, bad habits broken
Attend Master assigns progressively harder work
Consolidate Journeyman piece: integration of skills
Remember The craft passed on, the lineage

Cut beats

Paragraphs that were true but slowed the arc of The Natural Framework.

What changes across rows: The clock speed. The substrate. The vocabulary.

Convergent solutions: Consolidation showed the pipeline converges on the Transformer architecture. That was one row’s evidence. The tables add fifty-eight more. The same pattern appears wherever the constraint appears. A narrow basin of convergent solutions: systems under finite capacity with selection pressure arrive at the same six-step shape. The timescale is irrelevant. The steps are not.

Nature solved it: The immune system runs all six steps with no central coordinator. Evolution runs bright filter, attend, and consolidate despite dim perceive and structure, and it works over megayears.

Linked lists

The rows link forward. Each Remember is a pointer to another row’s Perceive. Here are chains we found.

The creative chain: neurons → cognition → writing → publishing → reading → cognition (loop). Cortical storage becomes what you perceive on screen. Publishing becomes what you read as research. The backlist becomes a book a reader opens. Output becomes input.

The career chain: birth → school (broken) → college (broken) → hiring (broken) → promotion → prestige. Each node’s dim cells compound into the next. School’s filter has no competitive inhibition (age-based promotion). College’s attend is GET * (GPA, no diversity across skills). Hiring inherits unstructured inputs. Dim cells propagate across nodes, not just downstream within a row.

The knowledge chain: science → curricula → education → cognition → writing → science (loop). Papers consolidate into textbooks. Textbooks become curricula. Curricula shape what students perceive. Students become researchers who observe new phenomena. The loop closes.

The infrastructure chain: evolution → neurons → cognition → writing code → computation → inference → agent → writing code (loop). The genome encodes the brain. The brain writes code. Code runs on hardware. Hardware runs inference. Inference powers agents. Agents write more code.

The medicine chain: science → clinical trials → prescribing → therapy → changed behavior. Papers become hypotheses. Approved drugs become options on the formulary. The patient record becomes the therapist’s intake.

The story chain: oral tradition → mythology → religion → law. Tales become myths. Myths become doctrine. Doctrine becomes canon law. Canon law becomes common law. The longest chain in the list: four domains from campfire to courtroom.

The book chain: oral tradition → translation → publishing → librarianship → education → cognition (loop). The myth gets translated. The translation gets published. The book gets shelved. The student opens it.

The evidence chain: forensics → law → precedent → law school → practice (loop). The forensic report enters the courtroom. The decision becomes precedent. Precedent becomes curriculum. Curriculum shapes the next lawyer’s practice.

The land chain: cartography → urban planning → architecture → the built city. The map becomes the zoning plan. The plan constrains the architect. The building constrains the next map.

The seed chain: gardening ↔ seed breeding (loop). The seed library feeds the breeder’s trial plots. The new cultivar feeds next year’s garden. The shortest chain in the list: two nodes, one loop.

The record chain: journalism → documentary. The archive becomes the source material. The released film becomes what the next journalist perceives as context.

Any composable unit of competition for selection works this way. Thirty-six rows here, twenty-four in the main post, and there are always more.
